message:
perf(runtime,net,http): migrate reactor backends and infrastructure maps from BTreeMap to HashMap

All six reactor backends (epoll, io_uring, kqueue, lab, macos/kqueue,
windows/iocp) used BTreeMap for their token→registration and fd→token
maps. Token and fd lookups are the hottest path in the I/O driver —
every poll turn performs multiple lookups — and none require sorted
iteration. HashMap gives O(1) amortized instead of O(log n).

Reactor backends:
- epoll.rs: ReactorState.tokens and fds → HashMap; Entry import
  switched from btree_map to hash_map; comment updated to say
  "HashMap lookup" instead of "BTreeMap lookup"
- io_uring.rs: registrations → HashMap
- kqueue.rs: registrations → HashMap
- lab.rs: LabInner.sockets → HashMap; BTreeMap removed from imports
- macos.rs: KqueueReactor.registrations → HashMap; ASCII diagram
  updated to say "HashMap<Token, info>"
- windows.rs: IocpReactor.registrations → HashMap

HTTP connection pool (pool.rs):
- HostPool.connections: BTreeMap<u64, _> → HashMap (connection IDs
  have no meaningful order)
- Pool.hosts: BTreeMap<PoolKey, _> → HashMap

DNS cache (cache.rs):
- DnsCache.ip_cache: BTreeMap<String, CacheEntry<LookupIp>> → HashMap
- evict_expired_locked() and find_oldest_key() parameter types updated

Distributed recovery (recovery.rs):
- RecoveryCollector.seen_esi: BTreeSet<u32> → HashSet
- StateDecoder.seen_esi: BTreeSet<u32> → HashSet
- contributing replica dedup: collect::<BTreeSet<_>> → HashSet

Runtime state (state.rs):
- snapshot() obligations_by_task: BTreeMap → HashMap
- cancel_region_tree() region_reasons: BTreeMap → HashMap

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
